figures.caption: Figure 2: Overall architecture of the proposed GUIDE framework.GUIDE employs progressive unrolling to inject multigranularity features from the 3D geometric encoder (left) into early LLM layers (middle) via a context-aware gate (right), enabling the model to gradually learn 2D-to-3D transitions and fetch geometric details on demand.
Table 1: The performance on VSI-Bench. We integrate recent general and spatial-enhanced models for comprehensive comparison. * denotes training using the S1+S2 dataset configuration from VG-LLM.
Table 2: Comparison of different models on VSI-Bench [41], MMSI-Bench [43], ViewSpatial [24], and CV-Bench [54].

Table 5: Comprehensive ablation study of injection layers and context-aware gating components on VSI-Bench. 𝐺𝑠𝑒𝑚 and 𝐺𝑔𝑙𝑜 denote the token-wise semantic gate and layer-wise global gate, respectively. Layers = 0 corresponds to the baseline (Input-Level Fusion).
